explicit_text: I was recruited in the Philippines back in 1962. Joining the service was a great opportunity for me to come to the United States. At that time, the Navy was the only branch recruiting Filipino citizens, so that’s why I chose it. My early days in the Philippines were filled with pressure because the examinations were very strict, both mentally and physically.

explicit_text: I served during the Vietnam War, specifically assigned to Cam Ranh Bay in Vietnam. Although we were in a combat area, my unit was mostly in support roles and we never engaged directly in firefights. We did come under mortar attacks several times at night, which was quite intense. Serving during that time was challenging but I was fortunate not to have any casualties in my unit.

explicit_text: Cam Ranh Bay in Vietnam was where I was stationed during the Vietnam War. We rotated there every two weeks from our home port, supporting Patrol Squadron 3. explicit_text: I joined the United States Navy in 1962 after being recruited in the Philippines. The Navy was the only branch recruiting Filipino citizens at that time.
